mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a win rate above 50% across 50 historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals supply-side bottlenecks and profit margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases do not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns, with directional accuracy below 45% over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing below 2.0 percent eliminates real yield drag on equity valuations and triggers algorithmic portfolio rebalancing into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of periods following a FEDFUNDS drop below 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates indicates structural labor market deterioration that suppresses corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a positive 60-day return win rate exceeding 55% across all post-2000 observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly increases corporate revenue expectations and equity cash flow projections, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 5-day S&P 500 direction, with a rank information coefficient consistently below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid capacity expansion and margin improvement, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration during post-shock recovery periods fails to predict positive 20-day S&P 500 returns, with directional accuracy below 48% over a 15-year sample.
